generatedSentence: A solitary, minimalistic gardener, living within domestic walls, often uncorks pistachio nuts, a warm reminder of his grandmom's jubilee. The knowing islander's warnings about far nightwalkers had long been neglected, a vexing shadow on his purple winters, but a tinder of resolve ignited, prompting him to start a list, observing the geese. Suddenly, a battle-hardened blacksmith, his face grouchy, his armor necklaced with strange items, visited the gardener, revealing an orphanage full of gamers who flunked training against monsters. The intake of knowledge about their deceased leader, a runner known for ultimate valor, spurred an anger that juiced their desire to elevate the nationals. They lived shacked by a fortress. The blacksmith, holding a hockey stick instead of a hammer, led them driving a hoverboard towards the fortress, a polymorphic hornet ray gun anchored to his back. The gamers, on a track adjacent to an okapi, battled the nightwalkers who advanced with hackles raised, their shadows stretching. The lioness polymorphed, her octave roar piercing the oceanic air, as the blacksmith forechecked with a mighty kick off. With thunderous kudos and vivat, the nightwalkers were beaten, their ultimate defeat summed up by a limping knucklehead. The gardener, now a harvester, lucubrated notes by his bookshelves as the sunset cast a purple glow. The orphanage, no longer needing funding or entertainments, embraced nature, free from the random astrology that once plagued their kalmuck, a final wonderbra of hope.

The knowing islander's warnings about far nightwalkers had long been neglected, a vexing shadow on his purple winters, but a tinder of resolve ignited, prompting him to start a list, observing the geese.

The intake of knowledge about their deceased leader, a runner known for ultimate valor, spurred an anger that juiced their desire to elevate the nationals. They lived shacked by a fortress.

The blacksmith, holding a hockey stick instead of a hammer, led them driving a hoverboard towards the fortress, a polymorphic hornet ray gun anchored to his back. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A grizzled blacksmith, wearing a heavy, scuffed leather apron over a chrome chest plate and wielding a plasma-charged hockey stick. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A solitary gardener in a futuristic greenhouse, tending to glowing plants, looking pensively at a holographic list. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The blacksmith, with a stern expression, gestures towards a group of young, determined gamers inside a desolate orphanage, a fortress looming in the distance. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The blacksmith, mounted on a sleek hoverboard, leads the gamers and a giant polymorphic lioness charging across a futuristic, alien landscape towards the ominous fortress, battling shadowy nightwalkers. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The gardener, now appearing as a wise harvester, sits calmly amidst bountiful, glowing crops at sunset, observing a peaceful landscape with the defeated nightwalkers retreating in the distance.

definitions.definition: A large ruminant mammal, Okapia johnstoni, found in the rainforests of the Congo, related to the giraffe, but with a much shorter neck, a reddish brown coat and zebra-like stripes on its hindquarters.
A person who plays any kind of game.
A person whose hobby is video games.
A person who games the system.
(obsolete) A gambler
One who gardens; one who grows plants or cultivates a garden.
definitions.example: "Ponder the fact that God has made you a gardener, to root out vice and to plant virtue." — St. Catherine of Siena

definitions.definition: An utterance of the interjection vivat.
A cry wishing someone long life and prosperity.
(Jewish history) A special year of emancipation supposed to be kept every fifty years, when farming was abandoned and Hebrew slaves were set free.
A 25th, 40th, 50th, 60th or 70th anniversary.
A special year (originally held every hundred years, then fifty, and then fewer) in which remission from sin could be granted as well as indulgences upon making a pilgrimage to Rome.
A time of celebration or rejoicing.
An occasion of mass manumission from slavery.
A period of fifty years; a half-century.
To open (a bottle or other container sealed with a cork or stopper) by removing the cork or stopper from.
definitions.example: uncork a bottle of wine

definitions.definition: To release.
Exhibiting or relating to minimalism.
Someone or something that reminds.
definitions.example: He left a note as a reminder to get groceries.

definitions.definition: Writing that reminds of open payments.
definitions.example: She ignored first the reminder of 80 cents. At the end, she was sentenced to pay 200 euros!

definitions.definition: Having a covering of small knobs.
A kind of shaggy cloth resembling bearskin.
Praise; accolades.
definitions.example: The talented, young playwright received much kudos for his new drama.

definitions.definition: Credit for one's achievements.
A house servant; a maid; a household worker.
A domestic dispute, whether verbal or violent
definitions.example: 2005: Bellingham-Whatcom County Commission Against Domestic Violence, Domestic Violence in Whatcom County (read on the Whatcom County website athttps//web.archive.org/web/20060618212243/http://www.co.whatcom.wa.us/boards/dv_whatcom042505.pdf on 20 May 2006) - The number of “verbal domestics” (where law enforcement determines that no assault has occurred and where no arrest is made), decreased significantly.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the home.
Of or relating to activities normally associated with the home, wherever they actually occur.
definitions.example: domestic violence;  domestic hot water

definitions.definition: (of an animal) Kept by someone, for example as a farm animal or a pet.
Internal to a specific country.
Tending to stay at home; not outgoing.
To add together.
To give a summary of.
Small dry sticks and finely-divided fibrous matter etc., used to help light a fire.
To set fire to; torch.
A strong feeling of displeasure, hostility or antagonism towards someone or something, usually combined with an urge to harm.
definitions.example: You need to control your anger.

definitions.definition: Pain or stinging.
To cause such a feeling of antagonism in.
definitions.example: He who angers you conquers you.

definitions.definition: To become angry.
definitions.example: You anger too easily.

definitions.definition: To informally execute by necklacing; by setting on fire a petrol-filled rubber tyre which has been put around the bound victim's neck.
A rampart of earth, stones etc. built up for defensive purposes.
A structure built for defense surrounding a city, castle etc.
definitions.example: The town wall was surrounded by a moat.

definitions.definition: Each of the substantial structures acting either as the exterior of or divisions within a structure.
definitions.example: We're adding another wall in this room during the remodeling.  The wind blew against the walls of the tent.

definitions.definition: A point of desperation.
A point of defeat or extinction.
An impediment to free movement.
definitions.example: A wall of police officers met the protesters before they reached the capitol steps.

definitions.definition: A type of butterfly (Lasiommata megera).
(often in combination) A barrier.
definitions.example: a seawall;  a firewall

definitions.definition: A barrier to vision.
Something with the apparent solidity and dimensions of a building wall.
definitions.example: a wall of sound;  a wall of water

definitions.definition: A divisive or containing structure in an organ or cavity.
(auction) A fictional bidder used to increase the price at an auction.
A doctor who tries to admit as few patients as possible.
A line of defenders set up between an opposing free-kick taker and the goal.
A personal notice board listing messages of interest to a particular user.
To enclose with, or as if with, a wall or walls.
definitions.example: He walled the study with books.

definitions.definition: To boil.
To well, as water; spring.
A spring of water.
A kind of knot often used at the end of a rope; a wall knot or wale.
To make a wall knot on the end of (a rope).
The place where water, air or other fluid is taken into a pipe or conduit; opposed to outlet.
The beginning of a contraction or narrowing in a tube or cylinder.
The quantity taken in.
definitions.example: the intake of air

definitions.definition: An act or instance of taking in.
definitions.example: an intake of oxygen or food

definitions.definition: The people taken into an organisation or establishment at a particular time.
definitions.example: the new intake of students

definitions.definition: A tract of land enclosed.
Any kind of cheat or imposition; the act of taking someone in.
To take in or draw in; to bring in from outside.
A person who lives on an island.
A distinct physical object.
definitions.example: Tweezers are great for manipulating small items.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object that can be picked up for later use.
A line of text having a legal or other meaning; a separate particular in an account.
definitions.example: In response to the first item, we deny all wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: (psychometrics) A question on a test, which may include its answers.
definitions.example: The exam has 100 items, each of which includes a correct response and three distractors.

definitions.definition: A matter for discussion in an agenda.
definitions.example: The first item for discussion is the budget for next year's picnic.

definitions.definition: Two people who are having a relationship with each other.
definitions.example: Jack and Jill are an item.

definitions.definition: A short article in a newspaper.
definitions.example: an item concerning the weather

definitions.definition: A hint; an innuendo.
To make a note of.
A deciduous tree (Pistacia vera) grown in parts of Asia for its drupaceous fruit.
The nutlike fruit of this tree.
(color) A pale green colour, like that of a pistachio seed.
A person who forges iron.
A person who shoes horses.
A blackish fish of the Pacific coast (Chromis punctipinnis).
To work as a blacksmith.
Of a student, to fail a class; to not pass.
definitions.example: He flunked math, again.

definitions.definition: Of a teacher, to deny a student a passing grade.
definitions.example: Unsatisfied with Fred's progress, the teacher flunked him.

definitions.definition: To shirk (a task or duty).
To back out through fear. (Commonly in the phrase 'flunk it', the 'it' referring to a specific task avoided; sometimes without specific reference describing a person's attitude to life in general.)
A terrifying and dangerous creature.
A bizarre or whimsical creature.
definitions.example: The children decided Grover was a cuddly monster.

definitions.definition: A cruel or antisocial person, especially a criminal.
definitions.example: Get away from those children, you meatheaded monster!

definitions.definition: A horribly deformed person.
A badly behaved child, a brat.
definitions.example: Sit still, you little monster!

definitions.definition: Something unusually large.
definitions.example: Have you seen those powerlifters on TV? They're monsters.

definitions.definition: A prodigy; someone very talented in a specific domain.
definitions.example: That dude playing guitar is a monster.

definitions.definition: A non-player character that player(s) fight against in role-playing games.
To make into a monster; to categorise as a monster; to demonise.
To behave as a monster to; to terrorise.
To harass.
Act or instance of running, of moving rapidly using the feet.
definitions.example: I just got back from my morning run.

definitions.definition: Act or instance of hurrying (to or from a place) (not necessarily by foot); dash or errand, trip.
definitions.example: I need to make a run to the store.

definitions.definition: A pleasure trip.
definitions.example: Let's go for a run in the car.

definitions.definition: Flight, instance or period of fleeing.
Migration (of fish).
A group of fish that migrate, or ascend a river for the purpose of spawning.
A path taken by literal movement or figuratively
An enclosure for an animal; a track or path along which something can travel.
definitions.example: He set up a rabbit run.

definitions.definition: Rural landholding for farming, usually for running sheep, and operated by a runholder.
State of being current; currency; popularity.
Continuous or sequential
A flow of liquid; a leak.
definitions.example: The constant run of water from the faucet annoys me.

definitions.definition: (West Virginia) A small creek or part thereof. (Compare Southern US branch and New York and New England brook.)
definitions.example: The military campaign near that creek was known as "The battle of Bull Run".

definitions.definition: A quick pace, faster than a walk.
definitions.example: He broke into a run.

definitions.definition: A sudden series of demands on a bank or other financial institution, especially characterised by great withdrawals.
definitions.example: Financial insecurity led to a run on the banks, as customers feared for the security of their savings.

definitions.definition: Any sudden large demand for something.
definitions.example: There was a run on Christmas presents.

definitions.definition: Various horizontal dimensions or surfaces
A standard or unexceptional group or category.
definitions.example: He stood out from the usual run of applicants.

definitions.definition: In sports
A line of knit stitches that have unravelled, particularly in a nylon stocking.
definitions.example: I have a run in my stocking.

definitions.definition: The stern of the underwater body of a ship from where it begins to curve upward and inward.

The horizontal distance to which a drift may be carried, either by licence of the proprietor of a mine or by the nature of the formation; also, the direction which a vein of ore or other substance takes.
A pair or set of millstones.
(usually in the phrase 'do a runner') A quick escape away from a scene.
definitions.example: He did a runner after robbing the drugstore.

definitions.definition: A deserter.
A type of soft-soled shoe originally intended for runners.
Part of a shoe that is stitched to the bottom of the upper so it can be glued to the sole.
A part of an apparatus that moves quickly.
definitions.example: After the cycle completes, the runner travels back quickly to be in place for the next cycle.

definitions.definition: A mechanical part intended to guide or aid something else to move (using wheels or sliding).
An automobile; a working or driveable automobile.
definitions.example: Is that old Mercedes on the forecourt a runner? / No, it has no gearbox.

definitions.definition: A strip of fabric used to decorate or protect a table or dressing table.
definitions.example: The red runner makes the table so festive.

definitions.definition: A long, narrow carpet for a high traffic area such as a hall or stairs.
definitions.example: How about we put down a clear runner in the front hall.

definitions.definition: A part of a cigarette that is burning unevenly.
A long stolon sent out by a plant (such as strawberry), in order to root new plantlets, or a plant that propagates by using such runners.
A short sling with a carabiner on either end, used to link the climbing rope to a bolt or other protection such as a nut or friend.
A competitor in a poker tournament.
A restaurant employee responsible for taking food from the kitchens to the tables.
A leaping food fish (Elagatis pinnulatis) of Florida and the West Indies; the skipjack, shoemaker, or yellowtail.
(sports slang) An employee of a sports agent who tries to recruit possible player clients for the agent.
A rope to increase the power of a tackle.
A speedrunner.
An idea or plan that has potential to be adopted or put into operation.
definitions.example: This idea isn't a runner. Let's not waste any more time on it.

definitions.definition: A circular wooden vessel, made of hooped staves.
A kind of basket made especially from straw of rushes, especially for holding fish; by extension, the contents of such a basket or similar container, used as a measure of weight.
definitions.example: 1961 18 Jan, Guardian (cited after OED):

definitions.definition: A collection of items forming the equipment of a soldier, carried in a knapsack.
Any collection of items needed for a specific purpose, especially for use by a workman, or personal effects packed for travelling.
definitions.example: Always carry a good first-aid kit.

definitions.definition: A collection of parts sold for the buyer to assemble.
definitions.example: I built the entire car from a kit.

definitions.definition: The set of skills and abilities chosen for a playable character.
The standard set of clothing, accessories and equipment worn by players.
Clothing.
definitions.example: Get your kit off and come to bed.

definitions.definition: A full software distribution, as opposed to a patch or upgrade.
A drum kit.
To assemble or collect something into kits or sets or to give somebody a kit. See also kit out and other derived phrases.
definitions.example: We need to kit the parts for the assembly by Friday, so that manufacturing can build the tool.

definitions.definition: A kitten (young cat).
A kit fox.
A young skunk.
A school of pigeons, especially domesticated, trained pigeons.

A very small long-necked violin that came in a variety of shapes and configurations, meant to be carried in a pocket and intended for instructors (dancing masters) to carry to accompany their students.
To make the first kick in a game or part of a game.
definitions.example: The players kick off for the third quarter and the clock starts.

definitions.definition: To start; to launch.
definitions.example: Let's kick off this project with a planning meeting.

definitions.definition: To dismiss; to expel; to remove from a position.
definitions.example: I got kicked off the team after a string of poor performances

definitions.definition: To die or quit permanently.
definitions.example: It's a wonder that old dog hasn't kicked off yet.

definitions.definition: To shut down or turn off suddenly.
definitions.example: The washer was working fine until it kicked off in the middle of a cycle.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become more active.
definitions.example: The party kicked off when the third bottle of wine was opened.

definitions.definition: (ranching) To force the weaning of a bovine cow's calf by restricting the calf's access to its mother's udders. Used figuratively or literally.
definitions.example: A week after we kicked off her calf that cow was still bawling.

definitions.definition: To be overcome with anger, to start an argument or a fight.
definitions.example: When she called him a drunk, it was the last straw. He just kicked off.

definitions.definition: To have a fight or argument start.
definitions.example: It really kicked off in town when the team lost.

definitions.definition: A dead person.
definitions.example: The deceased was interred in his local churchyard.

definitions.definition: One who has died. In property law, the alternate term decedent is generally used in US English. In criminal law, “the deceased” refers to the victim of a homicide.
No longer alive, dead
Belonging to the dead.
definitions.example: The executor’s commission for winding up the deceased estate was 3.5%.

definitions.definition: One who has died. In property law, the alternate term decedent is generally used. In criminal law, “the deceased” refers to the victim of a homicide.
To take part in exciting romantic adventures.
Divination about human affairs or natural phenomena from the relative positions of celestial bodies.
A subject of a nation.
definitions.example: The diplomats were advised not to interact with any foreign nationals except on official duty.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A tournament in which participants from all over the nation compete.
definitions.example: After winning the regional tournament, the team advanced to the nationals.

definitions.definition: Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
The period of decay, old age, death, or the like.
Someone with dark skin, eyes and hair, seen as best suited to certain colors of clothing.
An appliance to be fixed on the front of a grate, to keep a kettle warm, etc.
To spend the winter (in a particular place).
definitions.example: When they retired, they hoped to winter in Florida.

definitions.definition: To store something (for instance animals) somewhere over winter to protect it from cold.
In the winter.
definitions.example: They ski winters in the Laurentians.

definitions.definition: A colour/color that is a dark blend of red and blue; dark magenta.
(colour theory) Any non-spectral colour on the line of purples on a colour chromaticity diagram or a colour wheel between violet and red.

Cloth, or a garment, dyed a purple colour; especially, a purple robe, worn as an emblem of rank or authority; specifically, the purple robe or mantle worn by Ancient Roman emperors as the emblem of imperial dignity.
definitions.example: to put on the imperial purple

definitions.definition: (by extension) Imperial power, because the colour purple was worn by emperors and kings.
Any of various species of mollusks from which Tyrian purple dye was obtained, especially the common dog whelk.
The purple haze cultivar of cannabis in the kush family, either pure or mixed with others, or by extension any variety of smoked marijuana.
Purpura.
Earcockle, a disease of wheat.
Any of the species of large butterflies, usually marked with purple or blue, of the genus Basilarchia (formerly Limenitis).
definitions.example: the banded purple

definitions.definition: A cardinalate.
To turn purple in colour.
To dye purple.
To clothe in purple.
Having a colour/color that is a dark blend of red and blue.
Not predominantly red or blue, but having a mixture of Democrat and Republican support, as in purple state, purple city.
(in Netherlands and Belgium) Mixed between social democrats and liberals.
Imperial; regal.
Blood-red; bloody.
(of language) Extravagantly ornate, like purple prose.
(of a sector, lap, etc.) Completed in the fastest time so far in a given session.
A recreational drug based on cough syrup, popular in the hip-hop community in the southern United States.
A person who gathers the harvest.
A machine that gathers the harvest.
A program or algorithm that gathers data from a source.
A North American butterfly, Feniseca tarquinius, whose larvae eat aphids and are the only entirely carnivorous caterpillars in North America
Any butterfly of the lycaenid subfamily Miletinae to which this belongs, which are all carnivores
A stick used to handle the puck in ice hockey, having a flat, angled blade at the end.
A stick used to handle the ball in field hockey, having a flat-faced hook at the end.
A playing card with the rank of seven.
A statistical trend in a graph of survey data in which most of the results are flat and then the graph suddenly peaks in a steep near-vertical direction.
Any of various grazing waterfowl of the family Anatidae, which have feathers and webbed feet and are capable of flying, swimming, and walking on land, and which are bigger than ducks.
definitions.example: There is a flock of geese on the pond.

definitions.definition: A female goose (sense 1).
The flesh of the goose used as food.
A silly person.
A tailor's iron, heated in live coals or embers, used to press fabrics.
A young woman or girlfriend.
An old English board game in which players moved counters along a board, earning a double move when they reached the picture of a goose.
Distant; remote in space.
definitions.example: He went to a far land.

definitions.definition: Remote in time.
definitions.example: the far future

definitions.definition: Long.
More remote of two.
definitions.example: He moved to the far end of the state. She remained at this end.

definitions.definition: Extreme, as measured from some central or neutral position.
definitions.example: They are on the far right on this issue.

definitions.definition: Extreme, as a difference in nature or quality.
Outside the currently selected segment in a segmented memory architecture.
definitions.example: far heap; far memory; far pointer

definitions.definition: To, from or over a great distance in space, time or other extent.
definitions.example: He built a time machine and travelled far into the future.

definitions.definition: Very much; by a great amount.
definitions.example: He was far richer than we'd thought.

definitions.definition: That which is vain, futile, or worthless; that which is of no value, use or profit.
Excessive pride in or admiration of one's own abilities, appearance or achievements.

A dressing table used to apply makeup, preen, and coif hair. The table is normally quite low and similar to a desk, with drawers and one or more mirrors on top. Either a chair or bench is used to sit upon.
A washbasin installed into a permanently fixed storage unit, used as an item of bathroom furniture.
Emptiness.
Any idea, theory or statement that is without foundation.
definitions.example: It is a vanity to say that if two stones are dropped from a tower, the heavier will experience the greater acceleration.

definitions.definition: A roving motion; course without definite direction; lack of rule or method; chance.
Speed, full speed; impetuosity, force.
The full range of a bullet or other projectile; hence, the angle at which a weapon is tilted to allow the greatest range.
An undefined, unknown or unimportant person; a person of no consequence.
definitions.example: The party was boring. It was full of randoms.

definitions.definition: The direction of a rake-vein.
Having unpredictable outcomes and, in the ideal case, all outcomes equally probable; resulting from such selection; lacking statistical correlation.
definitions.example: The flip of a fair coin is purely random.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to probability distribution.
definitions.example: A toss of loaded dice is still random, though biased.

definitions.definition: Pseudorandom; mimicking the result of random selection.
(somewhat colloquial) Representative and undistinguished; typical and average; selected for no particular reason.
definitions.example: A random American off the street couldn't tell the difference.

definitions.definition: (somewhat colloquial) Apropos of nothing; lacking context; unexpected; having apparent lack of plan, cause or reason.
definitions.example: That was a completely random comment.

definitions.definition: Characterized by or often saying random things; habitually using non sequiturs.
definitions.example: You're so random!

definitions.definition: To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.
definitions.example: He knew something terrible was going to happen.

definitions.definition: To be aware of; to be cognizant of.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted or familiar with; to have encountered.
definitions.example: I know your mother, but I’ve never met your father.

definitions.definition: To experience.
definitions.example: Their relationship knew ups and downs.

definitions.definition: To be able to distinguish, to discern, particularly by contrast or comparison; to recognize the nature of.
definitions.example: I wouldn't know one from the other.

definitions.definition: To recognize as the same (as someone or something previously encountered) after an absence or change.
To understand or have a grasp of through experience or study.
definitions.example: His mother tongue is Italian, but he also knows French and English.

definitions.definition: To have sexual relations with. This meaning normally specified in modern English as e.g. to ’know someone in the biblical sense’ or to ‘know Biblically.’
To have knowledge; to have information, be informed.
definitions.example: He knows about 19th century politics.

definitions.definition: To be or become aware or cognizant.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted (with another person).
To be able to play or perform (a song or other piece of music).
definitions.example: Do you know "Blueberry Hill"?

definitions.definition: The act or condition of having knowledge.
Possessing knowledge or understanding; intelligent.
Shrewd or showing clever awareness.
definitions.example: a knowing rascal

definitions.definition: Suggestive of private knowledge.
Deliberate
To provide an impetus for motion or other physical change, to move an object by means of the provision of force thereto.
definitions.example: You drive nails into wood with a hammer.

definitions.definition: To provide an impetus for a non-physical change, especially a change in one's state of mind.
definitions.example: My wife's constant harping about the condition of the house threatens to drive me to distraction.

definitions.definition: To displace either physically or non-physically, through the application of force.
To cause intrinsic motivation through the application or demonstration of force: to impel or urge onward thusly, to compel to move on, to coerce, intimidate or threaten.
(especially of animals) To impel or urge onward by force; to push forward; to compel to move on.
definitions.example: to drive twenty thousand head of cattle from Texas to the Kansas railheads; to drive sheep out of a field

To notice with care; to observe; to remark; to heed.
definitions.example: If you look to the left, you can note the old cathedral.

definitions.definition: To record in writing; to make a memorandum of.
definitions.example: We noted his speech.

definitions.definition: To denote; to designate.
definitions.example: The modular multiplicative inverse of x may be noted x-1.

definitions.definition: To annotate.
To set down in musical characters.
To record on the back of (a bill, draft, etc.) a refusal of acceptance, as the ground of a protest, which is done officially by a notary.
That which is needed or necessary; business; duty; work.
The giving of milk by a cow or sow; the period following calving or farrowing during which a cow or sow is at her most useful (i.e. gives milk); the milk given by a cow or sow during such a period.
(An) electric shock.
Electroconvulsive therapy.
To administer electroconvulsive therapy.
An idiot; a stupid or inept person
definitions.example: Don't stick that screwdriver into the live electrical outlet, you knucklehead!

definitions.definition: An endearing remark directed to siblings or one's own children; a child who is acting silly.
definitions.example: Come over here you knucklehead(s)! I'm going to tickle you!

definitions.definition: To walk lamely, as if favouring one leg.
(of a vehicle) To travel with a malfunctioning system of propulsion.
definitions.example: The bomber limped home on one engine.

definitions.definition: To move or proceed irregularly.
definitions.example: The business limped through the recession

definitions.definition: To call, particularly in an unraised pot pre-flop.
To be inadequate or unsatisfactory.
To happen; befall; chance.
To come upon; meet.
The motion of one who limps.
The time of day when the sun disappears below the western horizon.
The changes in color of the sky at sunset.
The final period of the life of a person or thing.
definitions.example: one's sunset years

definitions.definition: Having a set termination date.
definitions.example: The tax increase legislation included a sunset clause requiring renewal to prevent the tax increase from expiring.

definitions.definition: The region where the sun sets; the west.
To phase out.
definitions.example: We'll be sunsetting version 1.9 of the software shortly after releasing version 2.0 next quarter.

definitions.definition: A shelf or shelves for storing books for easy visual reference.
To pay for.
definitions.example: He used his inheritance to fund his gambling addiction.

definitions.definition: To place (money) in a fund.
To form a debt into a stock charged with interest.
The action of the verb fund.
Money provided as funds.
definitions.example: The council is providing funding to the church to repair the roof.

definitions.definition: To pressure the puck carrier for the opposing team
The beginning of an activity.
definitions.example: The movie was entertaining from start to finish.

definitions.definition: A sudden involuntary movement.
definitions.example: He woke with a start.

definitions.definition: The beginning point of a race, a board game, etc.
definitions.example: Captured pieces are returned to the start of the board.

definitions.definition: An appearance in a sports game, horserace, etc., from the beginning of the event.
definitions.example: Jones has been a substitute before, but made his first start for the team last Sunday.

definitions.definition: A young plant germinated in a pot to be transplanted later.
An initial advantage over somebody else; a head start.
definitions.example: to get, or have, the start

definitions.definition: A vampire.
To smash.
(of birds) To move in a side-to-side motion and descend (lose altitude) before landing. Cf wiffle, whiffle.
definitions.example: The geese waffled as they approached the water.

definitions.definition: To speak or write vaguely and evasively.
To speak or write at length without any clear point or aim.
To vacillate.
To rotate (one's hand) back and forth in a gesture of vacillation or ambivalence.
Having a waffle-like pattern of intersecting creases.
Having a temperature slightly higher than usual, but still pleasant; mildly hot.
definitions.example: The tea is still warm.

definitions.definition: Caring and friendly, of relations to another person.
definitions.example: We have a warm friendship.

definitions.definition: Having a color in the red-orange-yellow part of the visible electromagnetic spectrum.
Close, often used in the context of a game in which "warm" and "cold" are used to indicate nearness to the goal.
Fresh, of a scent; still able to be traced.
Communicating a sense of comfort, ease, or pleasantness
definitions.example: a warm piano sound

definitions.definition: Ardent, zealous.
definitions.example: a warm debate, with strong words exchanged

definitions.definition: Well off as to property, or in good circumstances; rich.
Requiring arduous effort.
(sometimes attributive) In billiards and similar games, a stroke causing a ball to follow another ball after hitting it.
definitions.example: a follow shot

definitions.definition: The act of following another user's online activity.
To go after; to pursue; to move behind in the same path or direction.
definitions.example: Follow that car!

definitions.definition: To go or come after in a sequence.
definitions.example: B follows A in the alphabet.

definitions.definition: To carry out (orders, instructions, etc.).
definitions.example: Follow these instructions to the letter.

definitions.definition: To live one's life according to (religion, teachings, etc).
To understand, to pay attention to.
definitions.example: Do you follow me?

definitions.definition: To watch, to keep track of (reports of) some event or person.
definitions.example: I followed the incumbent throughout the election.

definitions.definition: To subscribe to see content from an account on a social media platform.
definitions.example: If you want to see more of our articles, follow us on Twitter.

definitions.definition: To be a logical consequence of something.
definitions.example: If you don't practise proper hygiene, illness is sure to follow.

definitions.definition: To walk in, as a road or course; to attend upon closely, as a profession or calling.
A fortified place; a large and permanent fortification, sometimes including a town; for example a fort, a castle; a stronghold; a place of defense or security.
A position that, if obtained by the weaker side, will prevent penetration by the opposing side, generally achieving a draw.
To furnish with a fortress or with fortresses; to guard, to fortify.
To trouble aggressively, to harass.
To annoy, irritate.
To cause (mental) suffering to; to distress.
To twist, to weave.
To be irritated; to fret.
To toss back and forth; to agitate; to disquiet.
Vexation
A prostitute's client.
A device or place to urinate and defecate: now usually a toilet or lavatory, but also a chamber pot or outhouse.
A Western man traveling in East Asia.
A male mule.
A stimulant, such as amphetamine, that increases energy and decreases appetite.
The upper portion of something
Someone with higher social standing
That which is higher, contrasted with the lower.
A spiritual passageway through which consciousness can reach a higher dimension.
At a higher level, rank or position.
Situated on higher ground, further inland, or more northerly.
(of strata or geological time periods) younger, more recent
Of or pertaining to a secondary school.
A tool used to moor a vessel to the bottom of a sea or river to resist movement.
An iron device so shaped as to grip the bottom and hold a vessel at her berth by the chain or rope attached. (FM 55-501).
The combined anchoring gear (anchor, rode, bill/peak and fittings such as bitts, cat, and windlass.)
Representation of the nautical tool, used as a heraldic charge.

Any instrument serving a purpose like that of a ship's anchor, such as an arrangement of timber to hold a dam fast; a device to hold the end of a bridge cable etc.; or a device used in metalworking to hold the core of a mould in place.
A marked point in a document that can be the target of a hyperlink.
An anchorman or anchorwoman.
The final runner in a relay race.
A point that is touched by the draw hand or string when the bow is fully drawn and ready to shoot.
A superstore or other facility that serves as a focus to bring customers into an area.
That which gives stability or security.
A metal tie holding adjoining parts of a building together.
Carved work, somewhat resembling an anchor or arrowhead; part of the ornaments of certain mouldings. It is seen in the echinus, or egg-and-anchor (called also egg-and-dart, egg-and-tongue) ornament.
One of the anchor-shaped spicules of certain sponges.
One of the calcareous spinules of certain holothurians, as in species of Synapta.
The thirty-fifth Lenormand card.
An anchorite or anchoress.
The act of neglecting.
The state of being neglected.
Habitual lack of care.
To fail to care for or attend to something.
definitions.example: to neglect duty or business;  to neglect to pay debts

definitions.definition: To omit to notice; to forbear to treat with attention or respect; to slight.
definitions.example: to neglect strangers

definitions.definition: To fail to do or carry out something due to oversight or carelessness.
